response.justification: The candidate brings valuable geospatial domain expertise through GISCollective and strong distributed systems experience, but has a critical gap in the primary requirement: modern C++ development (last significant C++ work ended in 2015, 10+ years ago). Recent career trajectory has shifted toward TypeScript/Node.js. While the demonstrated ability to learn across languages and the senior-level systems thinking are valuable, success would require rapid ramp-up in both modern C++ and routing/navigation-specific problem domains.
strengths: Geospatial domain expertise through GISCollective and OpenGreenMap projects, demonstrating hands-on understanding of location data systems
Senior-level distributed systems experience: microservices at scale (millions of requests/second), real-time data processing, asynchronous I/O and caching optimization
Proven ability to learn across multiple languages and domains throughout career spanning embedded systems, web, mobile, and infrastructure
concerns: Modern C++ expertise is stale with a 10-year gap since last significant C++ work (National Instruments 2014-2015); recent years heavily TypeScript/Node.js focused
Geospatial experience centers on data collection and mapping (GISCollective for point-of-interest mapping) rather than routing algorithms, optimization, or navigation guidance systems
No demonstrated experience building or operating critical data transformation pipelines; recent roles prioritize application-level development over systems-level infrastructure
